title: How to play EQOA: Frontiers
description: Currently the only live server for EQOA: Frontiers is Sandstorm. EQOAEmu is a planned server in the future.
Sandstorm:
### 1. Prepartion - Download the required files (provided externally)
- Download and install the PS2 emulator: [PCSX2 Download Page](https://pcsx2.net/downloads/)
- Download Sandstorm's pnatch file: [Sandstorm's Website](https://eqoa.live/)
    - Currently this is labeled as [eeee1fcc.pnatch](https://eqoa.live/eeee1fcc.pnach)
- PS2 BIOS: [BIOS Dump Instructions](https://pcsx2.net/docs/setup/bios/)
    > ⚠️ Most people are not able to do this. At your own risk, you can [search externally](https://www.emuparadise.me/roms/search.php?query=ps2+bios&section=all).
- ISO file: [ISO Dump Instructions](https://pcsx2.net/docs/setup/dumping/)
    > ⚠️ Most people are not able to do this. At your own risk, you can [search externally](https://romsfun.me/roms/playstation-2/everquest-online-adventures-frontiers.html).
     # You should have these 4 files:
        📁 Downloads/  
        ├── 📄pcsx2-vx.x.x-windows-x64-installer.exe      # PS2 Emulator  
        ├── 📄eeee1fcc.pnach                              # Sandstorm patch  
        ├── 📄ps2-023a-20080220.bin                       # BIOS file (can vary, but will be a .bin)  
        └── 📄EverQuest-Online-Adventures-Frontiers.iso   # Game ISO

### 2. Installing and setting up PCSX2
# Installation
- Run the installer (pcsx2-vx.x.x-windows-x64-installer.exe)
- Choose `Standard installation`
- Destination location can be anywhere you like, I.E the default `C:\\Program Files\\PCSX2`
# Setup
- Open your download directory with the required files above and the destination directory you chose directly above to move the required files over. 
  - Copy the 📄Sandstorm `.pnatch` file and paste into the 📂`cheats` folder, then go back to PSX2
  - Copy the 📄BIOS `.bin` file and paste into the 📂`bios` folder, then go back to PCSX2
  - Create a 📂`games` folder, copy the 📄EverQuest-Online-Adventures-Frontiers `.iso` file and paste it into it
# You should have these 3 folders with files in them:
        📁 PCSX2/
        ├── 📂 cheats/
        │   └── 📄 eeee1fcc.pnach
        ├── 📂 bios/
        │   └── 📄 ps2-023a-20080220.bin
        └── 📂 games/
            └── 📄 EverQuest-Online-Adventures-Frontiers.iso
- Launch PCSX2 and you'll be prompted with the Setup Wizard ![Setup Wizard 1](how_to_play/1)
    - Navigate to your destination directory, if not already, then choose the 📂`bios` folder, click "Next"
    - Click `Add` and select your📂`games` folder, click yes to scanning recursively (not important), click "Next"
    - Leave the controller setup to DualShock 2, click "Next"
    - Click "Finish"
> If you plan to dualbox, you will run this again with the portable option.


### 3. Configuring PCSX2 for EQOA - Frontiers
- In PCSX2, click `Settings` at the top and click `Emulation`
   - ❗Important - Turn on `Enable Cheats`
   - ❗Important - On the left - click `Memory Cards` > `Create` > Any name & `32 MB` > OK
        - Right click your memory card > `Use for Slot 1`, uncheck Slot 2
   - ❗Important - On the left - click `Network & HDD` > turn on `Enabled` > Set "Ethernet Device Type": `Sockets`, "Ethernet Device": `Auto`, close settings window
   - ❗Important - Right click the EQOA game > click `Properties`> On the left, click `Cheats` > Turn on Sandstorm DNAS cheats, close settings window
  - *Optional* visual settings:
    - Graphics > Apply Widescreen Patches 
    - Graphics > Apply No-Interlacing Patches
    - Graphics > Rendering > Anisotropic Filtering > 16x
  - # Map your controller settings
    - In PCSX2, click `Settings` at the top and click `Controllers`
    - Click Controller Port 1 > Map as needed > `Automatic Mapping` for your controller (recommended) or keyboard
       > 💡Tip: If you have a controller, set `USB Port 1` to "HID Keyboard (Konami)" so you can type with your PC's keyboard
### 4. Initial in game setup
- Double click the game to launch it
- Agree to EULA to proceed
- As prompted, format your memory card and prepare it
- Go to the `Network Settings`, use all default settings > `Add Setting` > `SCE/Ethernet Network Adaptor` > `Not Required` > `Auto` > `Auto` > Any setting name > Test connection (optional) > Go back to main menu
- Go to `Play` and use the network you created.
- Create your account or login to an existing account and you're ready to play!
